title: Contaminated-site barrier: domain-decomposition (FBPINN) PINN
governing_equations: c_t = D(x)\,c_{xx} + f,\ D=D_{soil}\ \text{outside}\ [0.45,0.55],\ D_{barrier}=0.1\ \text{inside};\ c^*=(1-e^{-t})\,\Psi(x)
expected_band: plume slowed by the low-D barrier (kink in c at each face); relative-L2 ~2e-1 on the CPU 2-channel lane (the coefficient-jump kink is the hard part; the strict per-subdomain-normalized FBPINN + GPU tighten it)

variants.note_en: 10× low-D barrier in [0.45,0.55]: the plume is slowed and c develops a kink at each barrier face.
variants.note_es: Barrera de baja D (10×) en [0.45,0.55]: el plume se frena y c desarrolla un quiebre en cada cara.

comparison.note_en: The naive plain PINN (no domain-decomposition) vs the domain-decomposition fix, both against the classical standard solution. Naive L2 = 19.2%, adapted L2 = 19.1%.
comparison.note_es: La PINN ingenua (sin domain-decomposition) frente a la corrección domain-decomposition, ambas contra la solución estándar clásica. L2 ingenua = 19.2%, adaptada = 19.1%.

estimate.question_en: How much does the cutoff wall delay the plume downstream? (remediation design)
estimate.question_es: ¿Cuánto retrasa el muro pantalla al penacho aguas abajo? (diseño de remediación)
estimate.why_en: The barrier's kink in the flux is exactly what the domain-decomposed net can represent; the delay is read off the reconstructed field.
estimate.why_es: El quiebre de flujo en la barrera es justo lo que la red por descomposición de dominio puede representar; el retraso se lee del campo reconstruido.

items.label_en: half-rise time at x=0.75 (past the barrier)
items.label_es: tiempo de media subida en x=0.75 (tras la barrera)
